revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:早馬神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hayama Shrine | image = File:Hayamajinja.JPG | coordinates = {{Coord|38.903056|N|141.638889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = * Shatomiya: Shukuura 75, Karakuwa-cho, Kesennuma City, Miyagi Prefecture * Okumiya: Magaritatsu, Karakuwa-cho, Kesennuma City, Miyagi Prefecture | deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|1=Ukanomitama}} | established = {{nihongo|Kenpō 5|建保5th year |1217}} | festivals = * {{nihongo|Reitaisai|festival}} (September 19) * {{nihongo|Shinkōsai|神幸祭}} (First Sunday of October) | prefecture= {{ill|Kesennuma|qid=Q235746|1=Kesennuma}} }}
'''Hayama Shrine''' ({{nihongo|早馬神社|はやまじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa, Miyagi|en|Karakuwa, Miyagi|qid=Q6368168|lt=Karakuwa|1=Karakuwa, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|en|Kesennuma|qid=Q235746|lt=Kesennuma|1=Kesennuma}}, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. established in {{nihongo|1217|建保5th year }}, it was founded by {{nihongo|Kajiwara Kagemasa|梶原景実}}, the elder brother of {{nihongo|Kajiwara Kagetoki|梶原景時}}, and remains deeply connected to the {{ill|Kajiwara clan|ja|梶原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara clan|qq=}}.
== Overview == The shrine has two primary sites: the Shatomiya (lower shrine) in the Shukuura area and the Okumiya (upper shrine) located atop Hayama Mountain. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as {{nihongo|"Hayama-sama"|早馬さま}} or {{nihongo|"Gongen-sama"|権現様}}.
According to legend, Hayama Shrine was established when Kagemasa left {{nihongo|Kamakura|鎌倉}}, witnessing the decline of his family and the deaths of {{nihongo|Minamoto no Yoritomo|源頼朝}} and {{nihongo|Kajiwara Kagetoki|梶原景時}}. Settling in the Karakuwa region, he built a shrine to enshrine the spirits of his family and to honor Yoritomo.
The Shatomiya site was originally located in the Ishihama area but was relocated to Shukuura following the destruction caused by the {{nihongo|Keichō Sanriku Tsunami|慶長三陸津波}} in 1611.
== Deities == The shrine's main deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080728}}, worshipped for blessings related to safe childbirth, child-rearing, and maritime safety.
== Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its tall cedar trees, which are believed to be centuries old. The two "Shitanihonsugi" (lower two cedars) remain significant landmarks, although the "Kaminihonsugi" (upper two cedars) were lost to storm damage in 1999.
== Events and festivals == * **March:** {{nihongo|Uramatsuri|浦祭}}, a maritime ritual praying for safety and a bountiful catch. * **September 19:** {{nihongo|Reitaisai|festival}}, featuring maritime {{nihongo|Mikoshi Togyo|神輿渡御}} (portable shrine processions) and rituals like tide purification. * **First Sunday of October:** {{nihongo|Shinkōsai|神幸祭}} and the Karakuwa Hayama Oyster festival, showcasing local seafood. * **Unscheduled:** {{nihongo|Hamabarai|浜祓い}}, rituals performed after maritime fatalities.
== East Japan Earthquake Impact == During the {{nihongo|Great East Japan Earthquake|東日本大震災}} in 2011, the Shatomiya suffered damage from the tsunami, which reached the shrine's elevated location. Restoration efforts followed, and the site has since been included as a pilgrimage stop in the "Tohoku Henro Project," a cultural initiative to commemorate disaster recovery.
== Access == * **Shatomiya location:** Shukuura 75, Karakuwa-cho, Kesennuma City, Miyagi Prefecture. * **Okumiya location:** Magaritatsu, Karakuwa-cho, Kesennuma City, Miyagi Prefecture. * Nearby landmarks include Hayama Mountain Observatory and Shukuura Port.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qid=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}}
